error_signature: localhost not allowed as redirect hostname
title: Google OAuth redirect URL issue with localhost
symptom: The OAuth Redirect URL for Google Drive setup in n8n is set to localhost, which is not accepted by Google. This prevents successful OAuth authentication.
not_this_if: Using a valid domain for OAuth setup
root_cause: Google's OAuth implementation requires a publicly accessible domain for redirect URLs. Using 'localhost' means the redirect cannot be recognized by Google's servers, leading to authentication failures.
fix_steps: Change the redirect URL in your Google Cloud Console to a publicly accessible domain.
If using Docker, set the WEBHOOK_URL environment variable to your domain.
Restart the n8n Docker container to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Update the redirect URL in Google Cloud Console and set the WEBHOOK_URL in your n8n Docker configuration.
